However, I found information on the plant Acacia roxburghii, which is a synonym for Acacia roxburghiana. 

Acacia roxburghiana is a plant species, commonly known as the Roxburgh's wattle, which is a shrub or small tree in the family Fabaceae.

The Roxburgh's wattle has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. 

The bioactive constituents of Acacia roxburghiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins.

The most common herbal preparations made with Acacia roxburghiana include decoctions, infusions, and powder, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections.
